steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.viventem: 'Viventem' is a present active participle meaning 'the living one'
hints.mortuis: 'Mortuis' is an adjective/participle in the ablative plural meaning 'the dead'

hints.to ask: 'To ask' is 'rogare/quaerere' — 'quaerere' can mean 'to ask' but here it means 'to seek'

steps.reveal: quaeritis = you seek / you are looking for (2nd pl. pres.)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'quaeratis' — 'quaeritis' is indicative

hints.queror, queri, questus sum, —: These are the parts of 'queror' (to complain) — a deponent verb
hints.quatio, quatere, quassi, quassum: These are the parts of 'quatio' (to shake)
hints.capio, capere, cepi, captum: These are the parts of 'capio' (to seize/take)

steps.reveal: viventem = the living one (acc. sing., substantive participle)

hints.nominative singular: Nominative would be 'vivens' — 'viventem' with -em is accusative
hints.genitive plural: Genitive plural would be 'viventium' — 'viventem' is acc. sing.

steps.target: present active participle used as a substantive noun
choices: present active participle used as a substantive noun
steps.reveal: viventem = the living one (participle of 'vivo' used as a noun)

hints.perfect passive participle: Perfect passive participle would end in -tum/-tus — 'viventem' has the -ent- of present participle
hints.gerundive: Gerundive would be 'vivendum' — 'viventem' is a present active participle
hints.adjective: While participles can function as adjectives, 'viventem' here is a substantive (used as a noun)

hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the parts of 'vinco' (to conquer) — note the 4th part is identical to 'vivo'
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the parts of 'video' (to see)
hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the parts of 'venio' (to come)

steps.reveal: cum mortuis = among the dead (abl. pl.)

hints.dative plural: 'Mortuis' looks the same in dative plural, but 'cum' governs the ablative
hints.nominative plural: Nominative plural would be 'mortui' — 'mortuis' is ablative after 'cum'
hints.genitive plural: Genitive plural would be 'mortuorum' — 'mortuis' is ablative

steps.reveal: Quid quaeritis = Why do you seek...?

hints.relative pronoun (which): 'Quid' can be relative, but here it opens a direct question — meaning 'why'
hints.indefinite pronoun (something): 'Quid' as indefinite follows 'si/ne' — here it is a direct interrogative
hints.interrogative pronoun (what thing): 'Quid' can mean 'what thing?' but here with 'quaeritis' + acc. object, it means 'why?'

steps.prompt: What literary contrast does this sentence employ?

hints.chiasmus: Chiasmus reverses word order (ABBA) — this is a direct contrast of living vs. dead
hints.tricolon: Tricolon has three parallel elements — this has two contrasting terms
hints.litotes: Litotes affirms by negating the opposite — this is a direct contrast, not a double negative

translation: Why do you seek the living among the dead?
